fragility_points: Weekend or headline-driven crypto volatility before April 20.
key_assumptions: BTC remains above 70000 specifically at the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close on April 20.
Binance produces a normal representative market print without disruptive anomaly.
No major macro or crypto-specific shock forces a 7%+ decline before resolution.
main_logical_chain: Read Polymarket rules to identify the governing source, timing, pair, and strict-above condition.
Check Binance direct spot and recent klines to measure current distance from the 70000 threshold.
Net the favorable price cushion against exact-minute timing and venue-specific failure risk.
Conclude that Yes is still likely but that 94% slightly overstates certainty, yielding a 90% estimate.
main_thesis: Yes is favored because Binance BTC/USDT is comfortably above 70000, but the market slightly overstates confidence for an exact-minute exchange-specific contract.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
My estimate is 90% Yes, below the market's roughly 94% implied probability.
source_quality_view: Source-of-truth ambiguity is low because rules are explicit, but evidence independence is only medium-low since the key direct evidence all points back to Binance-related surfaces.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves from one exact 12:00 ET 1-minute Binance candle close, so timing fragility remains real.
A roughly 7% cushion is meaningful but not unbreakable over several crypto trading days.
Exchange-specific wick or operational issues could matter because settlement is venue-specific.
strongest_supports: Binance spot at research time was about 75029.99, materially above the threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes in the fetched sample were all above 70000.
Polymarket rules make source-of-truth interpretation relatively clear.
timing_relevance: Timing is central because the contract settles on the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close on April 20 rather than on a daily close or broader average.
unresolved_ambiguities: Final settlement points to Binance chart surface rather than explicitly to the API endpoint.
Short-horizon BTC volatility before noon ET on April 20 remains unknowable.
what_would_change_view: I would move closer to market if BTC stays comfortably above 73k-74k into late April 19/early April 20; I would move lower if the cushion compresses, risk-off selling hits, or Binance shows dislocation.
